summary: Elder Patrick Kearon teaches that sustaining and serving in Church callings—whether visible or quiet—is a sacred exercise of faith, unity, and discipleship as we join the Savior in His work.
haiku:
Hands raised in faith’s light
Serving hearts become like Christ
About His business
key_points: Common consent is a meaningful act of agency, unity, and commitment to support those called to serve.
Church callings stretch, bless, and refine us as we learn charity, humility, patience, and faith through service.
Every calling can become the Lord’s business when accepted with a willing heart and a desire to bless His children.
scriptures_and_gospel_principles: Luke 2:49 — Jesus being about His Father’s business
Matthew 4:18–22 — Peter, Andrew, James, and John leaving their nets to follow the Savior
John 21:15–17 — The Savior’s invitation to feed His sheep
Mosiah 2:17 — Serving others is serving God
Doctrine and Covenants 26:2 — All things shall be done by common consent in the Church
Doctrine and Covenants 20:65 — Ordinations and callings are sustained by the Church
The doctrine of sustaining Church leaders and fellow Saints
The sanctifying power of service in Church callings
questions_to_ponder: What does it mean to truly sustain someone beyond raising my hand?
How have callings helped me grow in faith, humility, or love?
When have I seen the Lord work through imperfect servants, including myself?
How can I better support those serving in callings around me?
What would change in my attitude if I viewed every calling as being about the Savior’s business?
